description_long: This is a large, rectangular wooden storage box constructed from weathered planks, reinforced with diagonal wooden braces on each side for added stability. The box is open at the top, revealing a loosely packed layer of straw or hay inside, suggesting it is used for storing or transporting items that require cushioning or ventilation. The wood shows signs of aging, with visible knots, grain patterns, and some darkened or stained areas, giving it a rustic, utilitarian appearance. The construction is robust, with thick planks and no visible hardware such as hinges, locks, or handles. The interior is spacious and unobstructed, making it suitable for holding bulky or irregularly shaped objects. This type of box is commonly found in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or outdoor storage areas, where it can serve as a general-purpose container.
description: A large, rustic wooden storage box with open top and straw-filled interior, reinforced by diagonal braces and weathered planks.
description_view_0: End view showing the narrow side of the box with a diagonal wooden brace and visible straw inside.
description_view_1: Long side view displaying the full length of the box, diagonal brace, and more extensive weathering on the wood.
description_view_2: Opposite end view, similar to view 0, with a diagonal brace and visible straw, but slightly different wood grain and coloration.
description_view_3: Opposite long side view, showing a diagonal brace and darker, more weathered wood compared to view 1.
